bays.name: Bantam (TT) Patchbay
bays.description: Main patchbay connecting preamp outputs to A/D converter inputs. Normaled so signal flows from preamps to converters without any patching needed.

channels.source: AEA Ribbon Preamp Ch 9 (out for repair)
AEA Ribbon Preamp Ch 10 (out for repair)

littleLabsFaderBank.position: Between preamp outputs and A/D inputs
littleLabsFaderBank.notes: Pad signal when preamps are driven hard. Not needed for all sources.

tips: Plugging into the bottom row breaks the normal — your cable replaces the default preamp-to-converter connection.
No patching needed for default recording. The normals handle the standard preamp-to-A/D routing.
Bantam/TT cables are smaller than 1/4" — don't mix them up or you'll damage the jacks.
Push cables in firmly. Loose connections cause intermittent signal.
Pull all patch cables to restore default routing.

bays.name: TRS Patchbay
bays.description: Line-level patchbay for synths, outboard gear returns, and direct injection. Used to route line-level signals into the Bantam bay or directly to A/D inputs.
tips: Synth outputs are line level — loud enough to go straight to the A/D by patching from here to the Bantam bay bottom row.
To add preamp color to a synth, route from TRS bay to a preamp input instead.
The Prophet is especially hot — it can bypass the preamp entirely with plenty of level.

isoBoothConnectivity.notes: Wall plates in the iso booth pass through to the live room side. Connect from booth wall plate to snake or live room wall panel for routing to preamps.
